3146630.name: 電際亂戰ShoPaDen
3146630.description:
About This Game

This is a hard-hitting, fast-paced pixel-art 2D action game inspired by classic arcade action.
Take control of two characters with distinct attack styles and charge through a series of intense program blocks.

Each stage features unique environments and combat patterns that demand precise controls, quick reactions, and aggressive play.
Defeat enemies, collect encrypted data, and master the action as the difficulty steadily ramps up.

Designed with controller and Steam Deck play in mind, the game delivers tight, responsive combat for players who enjoy a real challenge.

Story

The story takes place on Earth —
but not the Earth as we know it.

One day, all electrical devices suddenly turned into ticking time bombs.

The cause was unknown.
Global communication collapsed, and nearly every device that relied on electricity was instantly destroyed.
Conspiracy theories spread rapidly, while some governments blamed hostile nations and prepared for war.
Human civilization was pushed back more than a century, and many people found life without modern technology unbearable.

Amid the chaos,
an unknown genius named Landell Vesta uncovered the truth.

The culprit was a form of electronic life —
a digital organism capable of traveling through electric currents and overloading any device it touched.
Even something as simple as a toaster could be reduced to ashes.

Landell managed to capture one of these entities and confine it within a sandbox environment.
However, even the most powerful conventional computers were unable to fully analyze its structure.

To go further, he created a device that could digitize living beings,
and made slight modifications to the virus itself, allowing entry into its internal code.

At that moment,
a mysterious girl named Muer Zai Longoria stepped forward to assist him.

Together, they dive into the heart of the virus —
and begin a reckless battle within its digital world.

— — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — —

— — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — — —

HUD
